            <nutrition_note>Balanced choice featuring complex carbs, fiber, healthy fats, and protein. Per serving: roughly 480–520 kcal; protein 15–20 g; carbs 40–50 g; fat 22–28 g; fiber 7–9 g. Lemon adds vitamin C; avocado provides potassium and monounsaturated fats; egg boosts protein. Optional chili flakes offer a mild spice kick without adding significant calories.</nutrition_note>
          </Meal>
          <Meal>
            <name>Veggie Omelet — Sesame Twist</name>
            <short_description>Egg-based veggie omelet with bell pepper, onion, and cheddar, optionally finished with a sesame twist.</short_description>
            <meal_suggestion>Serve with a slice of whole-grain toast or a side of fruit to add fiber. For the sesame twist, sprinkle a teaspoon of sesame seeds on top; you can also swap in a lighter cheese if desired.</meal_suggestion>
            <ingredients>
              <item name="eggs" />
              <item name="bell pepper" />
              <item name="onion" />
              <item name="cheddar" />
            </ingredients>
            <nutrition_note>Protein-rich omelet from eggs and cheddar supports satiety and muscle repair. Vegetables provide fiber, vitamin C, and antioxidants; cheddar adds calcium and fat. If using a sesame twist, sesame seeds contribute healthy fats and minerals.</nutrition_note>
          </Meal>
        </Breakfast>
        <Lunch>
          <Meal>
            <name>Salmon Salad Wrap — Citrus Fresh Style</name>
            <short_description>Protein-packed salmon yogurt salad wrapped in a whole-wheat tortilla with celery and greens.</short_description>
            <meal_suggestion>Mix salmon with Greek yogurt and diced celery; fill a whole-wheat tortilla with greens to assemble a crunchy, protein-forward wrap. Add a splash of lemon juice for the citrus lift if desired.</meal_suggestion>
            <ingredients>
              <item name="canned salmon" />
              <item name="Greek yogurt" />
              <item name="celery" />
              <item name="whole-wheat tortilla" />
              <item name="greens" />
            </ingredients>
            <nutrition_note>Protein-rich wrap with omega-3s from canned salmon and calcium from Greek yogurt. Includes fiber from whole-wheat tortilla and greens plus crunchy celery. Be mindful of sodium in canned salmon; citrus brightness can be added with a squeeze of lemon for the Citrus Fresh Style.</nutrition_note>
          </Meal>
          <Meal>
            <name>Tuna Pasta Salad — Citrus Fresh Bake</name>
            <short_description>A bright, protein-forward pasta salad with tuna, cucumber, and tomatoes dressed in olive oil—easy to prepare and versatile.</short_description>
            <meal_suggestion>Cook pasta until al dente, drain. In a bowl, fold in drained canned tuna, diced cucumber, and halved cherry tomatoes. Drizzle with olive oil and toss to coat. Chill for a refreshing cold salad or serve warm. Optional: add a squeeze of citrus juice or zest to echo the Citrus theme.</meal_suggestion>
            <ingredients>
              <item name="pasta" />
              <item name="canned tuna" />
              <item name="cucumber" />
              <item name="cherry tomatoes" />
              <item name="olive oil" />
            </ingredients>
            <nutrition_note>Excellent protein source from tuna and a balanced portion of carbohydrates from pasta. Olive oil provides heart-healthy fats; vegetables add fiber and micronutrients. If using canned tuna, choose low-sodium or rinse to reduce sodium.</nutrition_note>
          </Meal>
          <Meal>
            <name>Mediterranean Grain Bowl — Maple-Kissed Wrap</name>
            <short_description>Mediterranean grain bowl with quinoa, chickpeas, cucumber, tomato, feta, and olive oil; protein- and fiber-rich with a calcium boost.</short_description>
            <meal_suggestion>Serve as a bowl or wrap; brighten with lemon juice and fresh herbs (parsley, mint). For a dairy-free version, substitute feta with avocado or olives and reduce olive oil to lower calories.</meal_suggestion>
            <ingredients>
              <item name="quinoa" />
              <item name="chickpeas" />
              <item name="cucumber" />
              <item name="tomato" />
              <item name="feta" />
              <item name="olive oil" />
            </ingredients>
            <nutrition_note>High in plant-based protein and fiber from quinoa and chickpeas; includes calcium from feta and heart-healthy fats from olive oil; moderate sodium due to cheese.</nutrition_note>
          </Meal>
        </Lunch>
        <Dinner>
          <Meal>
            <name>Stuffed Bell Peppers — Garlic-Ginger Wrap</name>
            <short_description>Stuffed bell peppers filled with lean turkey, rice, and tomato sauce, topped with cheddar and baked until peppers are tender.</short_description>
            <meal_suggestion>For a lighter version, use brown rice and a smaller amount of cheese; pair with steamed greens or a side salad to boost fiber and micronutrients. You can batch-cook and refrigerate for quick meals.</meal_suggestion>

              <item name="bell peppers" />
              <item name="ground turkey" />
              <item name="rice" />
              <item name="tomato sauce" />
              <item name="cheddar" />
            </ingredients>
            <nutrition_note>Protein-rich stuffed peppers feature lean ground turkey, carbohydrates from rice, and calcium from cheddar. Peppers add fiber and vitamin C; using low-sodium tomato sauce and modest cheese helps manage sodium and saturated fat.</nutrition_note>

          <Meal>
            <name>Baked Salmon Plate — Mild-Spice Skillet</name>
            <short_description>Baked salmon with mild spices, lemon, mixed vegetables, and rice.</short_description>
            <meal_suggestion>Serving idea: 4–6 oz salmon, 1 cup cooked rice, 1–2 cups mixed vegetables; drizzle with lemon juice and a touch of olive oil or herbs. For more fiber, swap white rice for brown rice or quinoa.</meal_suggestion>

              <item name="salmon" />
              <item name="lemon" />
              <item name="mixed vegetables" />
              <item name="rice" />
            </ingredients>
            <nutrition_note>High-protein, omega-3-rich salmon; moderate carbs from rice; fiber and micronutrients from mixed vegetables; lemon adds vitamin C. Overall balanced and heart-healthy; suitable for portion-controlled dinners. Use minimal added salt if using spice blends.</nutrition_note>
          </Meal>
          <Meal>
            <name>Chicken Stir-Fry — Berry Boost Style</name>
            <short_description>Chicken stir-fry with mixed vegetables and brown rice in a light garlic-soy glaze. Berry Boost styling can be achieved by incorporating berries as a fresh or saucy accompaniment.</short_description>
            <meal_suggestion>Serve with a side of berries (e.g., blueberries or strawberries) or drizzle a berry sauce to honor the Berry Boost concept, while keeping the core protein, veggies, and brown rice for balanced nutrition.</meal_suggestion>
            <ingredients>
              <item name="chicken" />
              <item name="mixed vegetables" />
              <item name="brown rice" />
              <item name="soy sauce" />
              <item name="garlic" />
            </ingredients>
            <nutrition_note>Lean chicken provides high-quality protein. Brown rice and mixed vegetables supply fiber and essential nutrients; garlic adds flavor and potential immune-supporting properties. Soy sauce contributes sodium, so consider a low-sodium version to keep salt in check. The Berry Boost concept isn’t reflected by the listed ingredients—adding fresh or frozen berries on the side or in a berry-based sauce can boost antioxidants.</nutrition_note>
          </Meal>
        </Dinner>
      </Day>

Moderate to high sodium from soy sauce; consider low-sodium soy sauce or limiting portion. The Citrus Fresh Set can add vitamin C if paired with a citrus beverage or garnish.</nutrition_note>

            <name>Black Bean Wrap — Garlic-Ginger Bowl</name>

            <meal_suggestion>Great as a quick portable lunch. For more protein, add extra beans or a side of yogurt; for a flavor twist, try a light garlic and ginger drizzle or season the beans with garlic powder and ginger during warming.</meal_suggestion>
            <ingredients>
              <item name="whole-wheat wrap" />
              <item name="black beans" />
              <item name="avocado" />
              <item name="lettuce" />
              <item name="salsa" />
            </ingredients>
            <nutrition_note>Vegetarian wrap with protein from black beans, fiber from beans and whole-wheat wrap, and healthy fats from avocado. Estimated macros: protein 12–18 g, fiber 9–12 g, fats 9–15 g. Sodium varies with salsa; opt for low-sodium if watching salt.</nutrition_note>
          </Meal>
        </Lunch>
        <Dinner>
          <Meal>
            <name>Homemade Pizza Night — Mild-Spice Wrap</name>
            <short_description>Mild pizza-inspired wrap with whole-wheat crust, tomato sauce, mozzarella, mushrooms, and bell pepper — a fiber-rich, veggie-forward dish with calcium and protein.</short_description>
            <meal_suggestion>Serve as a 1-wrap portion; pair with a large green salad and water to create a balanced meal.</meal_suggestion>
            <ingredients>
              <item name="whole-wheat crust" />
              <item name="tomato sauce" />
              <item name="mozzarella" />
              <item name="mushrooms" />
              <item name="bell pepper" />
            </ingredients>
            <nutrition_note>Fiber from whole-wheat crust supports fullness; vegetables add vitamins, while mozzarella provides protein and calcium. Monitor sodium and saturated fat from cheese and sauce; for a lighter option, use part-skim mozzarella and a bit less cheese, and pair with a side salad to boost fiber.</nutrition_note>
          </Meal>
          <Meal>
            <name>Baked Salmon Plate — Maple-Kissed Bake</name>
            <short_description>Baked salmon with lemon, served with mixed vegetables and rice.</short_description>
            <meal_suggestion>Serve salmon with a squeeze of lemon over a bed of rice and accompany with steamed or roasted mixed vegetables for a balanced plate.</meal_suggestion>
            <ingredients>
              <item name="salmon" />
              <item name="lemon" />
              <item name="mixed vegetables" />
              <item name="rice" />
            </ingredients>
            <nutrition_note>High-protein meal with omega-3 fats from salmon; complex carbohydrates from rice; fiber and micronutrients from mixed vegetables; vitamin C from lemon. Low in added sugars unless a glaze is used.</nutrition_note>
          </Meal>
          <Meal>
            <name>Chicken Fajita Bowl — Sesame Wrap</name>
            <short_description>A protein-forward fajita bowl with chicken, peppers, onion, rice, and avocado, finished with sesame-inspired flavor.</short_description>
            <meal_suggestion>For more fiber or lower glycemic load, swap white rice for brown rice or cauliflower rice, and keep avocado. If making a wrap, use a sesame wrap with the same fillings and consider adding salsa or lime for brightness.</meal_suggestion>
            <ingredients>
              <item name="chicken" />
              <item name="bell peppers" />
              <item name="onion" />
              <item name="rice" />
              <item name="avocado" />
            </ingredients>
            <nutrition_note>Protein-rich chicken with peppers and onion provides fiber and micronutrients; rice supplies energy with carbohydrates; avocado adds heart-healthy fats and satiety. Sesame wrap variation can add extra fats and flavor. Salt content depends on seasoning.</nutrition_note>
          </Meal>
        </Dinner>
      </Day>
    </Person>"
